tossups.question: Master IW’s rendition of this scene in their last attributed work borrowed the tent-filled background from its depiction in Cranach the Elder’s Law and Gospel. The central object of this scene is unusually thick and erect in Fyodor Bruni’s rendition, which was displayed next to Bryullov’s The Last Day of Pompeii. The words “MYSTERIUM IUSTIFICATIONIS” appear above this scene in Hans Holbein’s Allegory of the Old and New Testaments, where it is the “type” to the Crucifixion’s “antitype”. Both Bronzino’s version above a door in the Palazzo Vecchio and the Sistine Chapel pendentive of this scene eschew a hero for a writhing mass of figures parting to show the central object, which canonically survived after being erected on Mount Nebo so it could be destroyed by King Hezekiah. For 10 points, name this scene in which Moses cures the Israelites by creating an icon with a draped metal snake.
tossups.question_sanitized: Master IW's rendition of this scene in their last attributed work borrowed the tent-filled background from its depiction in Cranach the Elder's Law and Gospel. The central object of this scene is unusually thick and erect in Fyodor Bruni's rendition, which was displayed next to Bryullov's The Last Day of Pompeii. The words "MYSTERIUM IUSTIFICATIONIS" appear above this scene in Hans Holbein's Allegory of the Old and New Testaments, where it is the "type" to the Crucifixion's "antitype". Both Bronzino's version above a door in the Palazzo Vecchio and the Sistine Chapel pendentive of this scene eschew a hero for a writhing mass of figures parting to show the central object, which canonically survived after being erected on Mount Nebo so it could be destroyed by King Hezekiah. For 10 points, name this scene in which Moses cures the Israelites by creating an icon with a draped metal snake.

tossups.question: This figure smiles cheekily as they poke their head in frame on Folio 135 of the Nuremberg Chronicle, which gave them a halo to match a person in a bishop’s hat.This figure was left entirely unpainted near the stone-holding subject in an incomplete work that was cut up before it was identified by Cardinal Fesch as a Leonardo. This figure sits next to Albert of Brandenburg in four portraits as a saint by Cranach the Elder. This figure is a spindly-legged silhouette in the colonnade on the right of a painting by Antonello da Messina that is framed by a stone arch. The story of this figure may have been borrowed wholesale from Androcles and appeared for the first time in the Golden <u>Legend</u>. This figure lies next to a sleeping dog in one of the Meisterstiche showing a saint writing diligently. For 10 points, name this creature that was tamed by having a thorn plucked from its paw.

This figure sits next to Albert of Brandenburg in four portraits as a saint by Cranach the Elder. This figure is a spindly-legged silhouette in the colonnade on the right of a painting by Antonello da Messina that is framed by a stone arch. The story of this figure may have been borrowed wholesale from Androcles and appeared for the first time in the Golden Legend. This figure lies next to a sleeping dog in one of the Meisterstiche showing a saint writing diligently. For 10 points, name this creature that was tamed by having a thorn plucked from its paw.

tossups.question: Christopher Scheurl wrote that one artist’s paintings of these animals were so realistic that birds tried to land on them. One of these animals drinks from a shallow puddle between a heron and a stork in a painting held at the Courtauld Gallery; in that painting, a large one of these animals lies on the ground in front of Adam. In another painting, a woman who is probably Sibylle, the wife of John Frederick the Magnanimous, steadies a (+) crossbow to shoot at one of these animals. These animals are featured in the foreground but not the midground of that painting, which is set near Hartenfels Castle. The artist’s winged snake emblem can be seen on a tree in the foreground of a painting in which a large party waits in a boat as these animals are chased into a river. For 10 points, name these animals which are (*) hunted by Frederick the Wise in a painting by Lucas Cranach the Elder.
tossups.question_sanitized: Christopher Scheurl wrote that one artist's paintings of these animals were so realistic that birds tried to land on them. One of these animals drinks from a shallow puddle between a heron and a stork in a painting held at the Courtauld Gallery; in that painting, a large one of these animals lies on the ground in front of Adam. In another painting, a woman who is probably Sibylle, the wife of John Frederick the Magnanimous, steadies a (+) crossbow to shoot at one of these animals. These animals are featured in the foreground but not the midground of that painting, which is set near Hartenfels Castle. The artist's winged snake emblem can be seen on a tree in the foreground of a painting in which a large party waits in a boat as these animals are chased into a river. For 10 points, name these animals which are (*) hunted by Frederick the Wise in a painting by Lucas Cranach the Elder.
tossups.answer_sanitized: stags [or bucks or roebucks or deer or roe deer]

tossups.question: It's not the Beast, but bees fly around one of this figure's seven heads in a woodcut by Hans Brosamer. The dead bodies of Aristotle and four other philosophers appear below this figure in a caricature as Hercules by Hans Holbein the Younger. This figure points to a book as blood falls on a self-portrait of an artist in the central panel of the Weimar Altarpiece. Because of a prophecy at the Council of Constance, this figure is often depicted with a swan. A Hans Baldung Grien woodcut of this figure as a saint inspired a lament to him by Albrecht Dürer. An artist represented by a winged serpent shared his printing presses with this man. This man appears in a black robe with a floppy hat in many portraits by his friend Lucas Cranach the Elder. For 10 points, Cranach also made 22 woodcuts for the Bible translation by what German reformer?
tossups.question_sanitized: It's not the Beast, but bees fly around one of this figure's seven heads in a woodcut by Hans Brosamer. The dead bodies of Aristotle and four other philosophers appear below this figure in a caricature as Hercules by Hans Holbein the Younger. This figure points to a book as blood falls on a self-portrait of an artist in the central panel of the Weimar Altarpiece. Because of a prophecy at the Council of Constance, this figure is often depicted with a swan. A Hans Baldung Grien woodcut of this figure as a saint inspired a lament to him by Albrecht Durer. An artist represented by a winged serpent shared his printing presses with this man. This man appears in a black robe with a floppy hat in many portraits by his friend Lucas Cranach the Elder. For 10 points, Cranach also made 22 woodcuts for the Bible translation by what German reformer?

tossups.question: Two answers required. Rebecca Zorach claims that one of these two figures functions as an identifying attribute of the other in a painting that depicts them at its center, elaborating on Edgar Wind’s analysis of that painting. One of these two figures holds a honeycomb while being attacked by bees and “complaining” to the other in a Lucas Cranach the Elder painting. In a (*) Jean-Antoine Watteau painting, several figures named for one of these characters fly above a group of travelers who embark for the other’s birthplace. These two figures kiss in the foreground of an allegorical painting by Agnolo Bronzino that depicts them alongside personifications of Folly and Time. The center of Sandro Botticelli’s La Primavera depicts, for 10 points, what goddess of love and her son?
tossups.answer: Venus AND Cupid [accept Aphrodite instead of Venus; accept Cupid Complaining to Venus; accept Venus, Cupid, Folly, and Time]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Venus AND Cupid [accept Aphrodite instead of Venus; accept Cupid Complaining to Venus; accept Venus, Cupid, Folly, and Time]

tossups.question: A Russian adaptation of this artistic scene shows one of its subjects and the face of Joseph Stalin against the background of a red square. Cranach the Elder depicted the protagonist of this scene dressed mostly in red with red hair, a broad-brimmed red hat, and white gloves. A bronze sculpture of this scene originally sat on a pedestal describing how “kingdoms fall through luxury” and was displayed opposite of Donatello’s (*) David by Cosimo de’ Medici. Most depictions of this scene include an old maid with a basket next to the major female subject, the latter of whom is carrying a sword. For 10 points, identify this scene depicted twice by Artemisia Gentileschi in which a Jewish girl decapitates an Assyrian general.
tossups.answer: Judith beheading Holofernes [or any equivalent answer in which Holofernes is slain by Judith; or Judith and the head of Holofernes; or any answer in which Judith is posing next to the corpse of Holofernes; prompt on partial answers such as death of Holofernes]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Judith beheading Holofernes [or any equivalent answer in which Holofernes is slain by Judith; or Judith and the head of Holofernes; or any answer in which Judith is posing next to the corpse of Holofernes; prompt on partial answers such as death of Holofernes]
tossups.question_sanitized: A Russian adaptation of this artistic scene shows one of its subjects and the face of Joseph Stalin against the background of a red square. Most depictions of this scene include an old maid with a basket next to the major female subject, the latter of whom is carrying a sword. For 10 points, identify this scene depicted twice by Artemisia Gentileschi in which a Jewish girl decapitates an Assyrian general.

tossups.question: What name is shared by a geologic province and namesake town in Labrador, a town and county in Iran famous for its namesake rugs as well as the Narej Fortress, and an Arab-Israeli town mentioned in the New Testament as being the site of the miraculous revival of the son of a widow depicted in art by Lucas Cranach?
tossups.question_sanitized: What name is shared by a geologic province and namesake town in Labrador, a town and county in Iran famous for its namesake rugs as well as the Narej Fortress, and an Arab-Israeli town mentioned in the New Testament as being the site of the miraculous revival of the son of a widow depicted in art by Lucas Cranach?

tossups.question: Methods used to decorate these things were adopted into printmaking by Daniel Hopfer, the inventor of etched prints. One of these objects whose decorations include images of Apollo chasing Daphne and slaying Python inside foliate scrolls was made by Étienne Delaune for Henry II of France and is now part of the Met’s extensive collection of these things. Jacob Halder’s namesake album depicts 29 designs for these things created at the Almain workshop, which exemplifies the elaborately gilded objects of this sort that were used on Accession Day festivities in Tudor England. Cranach’s The Crucifixion with the Converted Centurion contains a notable depiction of a 16th century object of this sort done in a style named after (*) Holy Roman Emperor Maximilian I. Components of these items included pauldrons, cuirasses, greaves, and mail skirts. For 10 points, name these things worn during jousts or battles by knights.
tossups.answer: suits of armour [or plate armor; prompt on protective coverings, attire, clothing, etc]
tossups.answer_sanitized: suits of armour [or plate armor; prompt on protective coverings, attire, clothing, etc]

tossups.question: Cristofano Allori is best known for a painting of this figure in orange in which he inserted himself in a self-portrait at the bottom. This figure was depicted in contemporary garb with a smug, sly smile and a red hat with large feather by Lucas Cranach the Elder. This figure is depicted androgynously in a blue toga, standing contrapposto outside of a pink tent in a painting by Andrea Mantegna. In one painting, this figure casts a dark shadow over the right side of her (*) face by holding her hand out in front of herself towards a candle. Gustav Klimt painted this figure wearing a thick gold necklace with bared breasts and half-closed eyes. This figure is almost always depicted accompanied by a maidswervant who is sometimes shown holding a general down. For 10 points, name this woman who creates Tarantino-esque spurts of blood as she saws off the head of Holofernes in a painting by Gentileschi.

This figure is depicted androgynously in a blue toga, standing contrapposto outside of a pink tent in a painting by Andrea Mantegna. This figure is almost always depicted accompanied by a maidswervant who is sometimes shown holding a general down. For 10 points, name this woman who creates Tarantino-esque spurts of blood as she saws off the head of Holofernes in a painting by Gentileschi.

tossups.question: In an alpine scene by this painter, red fir trees stand among blue-gray mountains beneath a pink sky. Lucas Cranach’s depiction of a nude Venus with a transparent shawl inspired this artist’s painting of a nude woman wearing a black hat, her pale body contrasting with the colorful background. Probably due to a tight budget, this artist of Winter Moonlit Night used both sides of 138 of his canvases, often rotating the canvas 90 degrees before painting the reverse. A mental breakdown during World War I led to a work by this artist in which he wears a dark blue uniform with red shoulder patches. In that self-portrait, this artist showed himself (*) smoking a cigarette while holding his left hand like a claw, in contrast to his severed right. This artist of Self-Portrait as a Soldier showed well-dressed men and women walking through brightly colored streets in works like Potsdamer Platz and Street, Berlin. For 10 points, name this member, along with Fritz Bleyl and Erich Heckel, of Die Brücke.

For 10 points, name this member, along with Fritz Bleyl and Erich Heckel, of Die Brucke.

tossups.question: This character stands next to a bare-chested black woman in a pink dress and headscarf in painting by Paolo Veronese. This figure is wearing white gloves with gold rings and a large red feathered hat in a work by Lucas Cranach the Elder. In one painting, a woman with a basket over her head runs behind this figure, who is walking with an olive branch; that work is by Botticelli. Another version of this character by Mantegna has red curly hair and stands in front of a (*) bed where a single foot is visible. Caravaggio’s depiction of this woman shows her maid as a wizened old woman carrying a cloth. A more youthful version of that maid, Abra, helps hold down this woman’s victim in one of another artist’s bloody paintings of her. For 10 points, name this woman painted by Artemisia Gentileschi, who is commonly depicted holding a sword and the head of Holofernes.

For 10 points, name this woman painted by Artemisia Gentileschi, who is commonly depicted holding a sword and the head of Holofernes.

tossups.question: A soldier in pink full body armor with a pink armored horse appears in one of Cranach the Elder’s versions of this scene, which divides the people in the scene into good and evil groups. This scene is the centerpiece of the predella to the San Zeno altarpiece by Mantegna. This scene is shown between Saint Sebastian and Saint Anthony in the (*) Isenheim Altarpiece, and it often includes the letters “INRI” above the central figure. Another painting of this scene includes a man in a black hat jumping a wall in the distance and uses shades of red and orange for the landscape behind three Breton women who surround the central figure. For 10 points, name this scene that is depicted in “Yellow” by Paul Gauguin.
tossups.answer: the crucifixion [or Jesus Christ on the cross; prompt on answers mentioning the “death of Jesus”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: the crucifixion [or Jesus Christ on the cross; prompt on answers mentioning the "death of Jesus"]

tossups.question: Two answers required. One of these people pees through the middle of a myrtle wreath held up by the other in a bizarre painting by Lorenzo Lotto. One of them, carrying a honeycomb, is covered in bees in a painting of these people by Cranach the Elder. A mischievous boy prepares to throw pink flowers over these two people as they make out and fondle in a painting whose background features a creepy little girl with lion's legs and a snake's tail. The elder of these people looks into a pink silk-draped mirror held up by the other in a Diego Velazquez painting now in Rokeby Park. For 10 points, name this mother-son pair depicted by Agnolo Bronzino along with Folly and Time.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Venus and Cupid [or Aphrodite and Eros]

bonuses.leadin: The 1695 bombardment of Brussels destroyed a massive set of Rogier van der Weyden panel paintings that depict Duke Herkinbald and a Roman Emperor exemplifying this quality. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The 1695 bombardment of Brussels destroyed a massive set of Rogier van der Weyden panel paintings that depict Duke Herkinbald and a Roman Emperor exemplifying this quality. For 10 points each:
parts: What quality is ascribed to a group of men on horseback, one of whom may depict Philip the Good, in a painted lower left panel missing since 1934?
Those destroyed van der Weyden panel paintings illustrated the “Justice” of Herkinbald and this Roman Emperor, who names a triumphal column north of the Roman forum.

Trajan is less flatteringly depicted in Renaissance paintings of this saint. Dürer, Cranach, and Pisanello executed the best-known depictions of this saint falling to his knees before a stag bearing a cross on its antlers.
parts_sanitized: What quality is ascribed to a group of men on horseback, one of whom may depict Philip the Good, in a painted lower left panel missing since 1934?
Those destroyed van der Weyden panel paintings illustrated the "Justice" of Herkinbald and this Roman Emperor, who names a triumphal column north of the Roman forum.

Trajan is less flatteringly depicted in Renaissance paintings of this saint. Durer, Cranach, and Pisanello executed the best-known depictions of this saint falling to his knees before a stag bearing a cross on its antlers.
answers: justice [or righteousness; or gerechtigheid; or rechtvaardige; accept The Just Judges accept, but DO NOT REVEAL, The Justice of Trajan and Herkinbald]

bonuses.leadin: This artist painted numerous depictions of Venus wearing nothing but jeweled chokers and large, elaborate hats sometimes decorated with pompoms. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this court painter to the Electors of Saxony, who also painted noblewomen wearing large hats. This artist signed his works with a winged snake holding a ruby ring and painted several portraits of Martin Luther.

Cranach the Elder depicted this woman holding up a sword and “dressed to kill” wearing jeweled necklaces and ornate hats. A popular “Power of Women” motif showed this woman with the head of Holofernes.

Several of Cranach’s Venuses pose wearing large hats while Cupid holds one of these objects and complains. A sign on a tree in one such scene moralizes on pleasure and pain, like Cupid’s pain due to his theft of this object.
answers_sanitized: Lucas Cranach the Elder [prompt on Cranach]

bonuses.leadin: In the Last Supper scene at the center of an altarpiece named for this town, an artist inserted a portrait of himself as a waiter and a portrait of a priest as one of the apostles. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this town inhabited by a man who is shown wearing a floppy black hat in a 1529 portrait. An artist from this town used a winged-serpent signature and painted Adam and Eve against a black background.

That portrait of Martin Luther is by this sometime Mayor of Wittenberg, who also painted The Stag Hunt of Elector Frederick the Wise. He is known as the “Elder” to distinguish him from his son, an artist of the same name.

The Lutheran Church officially venerates Cranach the Elder and this other artist on April 6. This artist became a rival of Cranach the Elder due to the success of his woodcuts, like Melencolia I and Knight, Death, and the Devil.
answers: Lucas Cranach the Elder [or Lucas Cranach der Ältere]

bonuses.leadin: Many Northern Renaissance artists, including Baldung and Cranach the Elder, painted depictions of a myth in which a whip-brandishing woman named Phyllis rides this man like a horse. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this man. Julius Held cited other depictions of this man in expensive jewelry to argue that a Rembrandt painting of a man with a gold chain depicts him rather than, as Simon Schama claimed, Apelles.

Depictions of Phyllis riding Aristotle were examples of this topos popular in Northern Renaissance art. This motif is also exemplified by paintings of Virgil in his basket and Judith with the head of Holofernes.
Aristotle, gesturing toward the ground, converses with his teacher, Plato, who points up at the sky, in this Raphael fresco depicting many ancient Greek mathematicians and philosophers.
answers: Aristotle [or Aristoteles] (The Dutch painting is Rembrandt’s Aristotle Contemplating a Bust of Homer.)
the “Power of Women” [or the Weibermacht; accept the battle for the trousers or the follies of love; prompt on descriptive answers like strong women or women overcoming men]

the "Power of Women" [or the Weibermacht; accept the battle for the trousers or the follies of love; prompt on descriptive answers like strong women or women overcoming men]

bonuses.leadin: In his book The Reformation of the Image, Joseph Koerner argues that this artwork’s depiction of the Last Supper, which shows Jesus dining with a group of Germans, “negates sacred geography, revealing church as activity, not place.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this altarpiece, the bottom panel of which depicts the crucifixion occurring between a certain preacher and his audience.
The Wittenberg Altarpiece was painted by this court painter to the Elders of Saxony, who depicted Frederick the Wise engaging in a stag hunt in another work.

Cranach’s (“CRAH-nuck’s”) religious paintings were heavily influenced by the theology of this good friend of his. Cranach provided a number of woodcut illustrations for this man’s German translation of the Bible.

packet.name: E (Penn State B, Berkeley A, and Harvard A)

bonuses.leadin: The blood of Christ takes an unlikely trajectory and lands on the head of this artist in the Weimar Altarpiece, which was completed by his son after his death. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who used an image of a winged snake with a ring in its mouth as his signature. He painted many scenes of Cupid and Venus, as well as a diptych of a nude Adam and Eve against a black background.

Cranach painted Frederick the Wise engaging in this action outside Hartenfels Castle. Anthony van Dyck painted Charles I and his horse during a break from this action, which is done “in the Snow” in a Bruegel painting.

In a Cranach painting titled for this emotion, a woman whittles away at a stick, ignoring the fifteen babies dancing next to her. Another 16th-century artwork titled for this emotion includes a truncated polyhedron with a faint skull marking on it and a magic square.
answers: Lucas Cranach the Elder [prompt on Cranach; do not accept “Lucas Cranach the Younger”]
hunting [accept word forms; accept stag hunt]

packet.name: E. Delaware A + Kentucky A + Cambridge A

hunting [accept word forms; accept stag hunt]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The blood of Christ takes an unlikely trajectory and lands on the head of this artist in the Weimar Altarpiece, which was completed by his son after his death. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this artist who used an image of a winged snake with a ring in its mouth as his signature. He painted many scenes of Cupid and Venus, as well as a diptych of a nude Adam and Eve against a black background.

Cranach painted Frederick the Wise engaging in this action outside Hartenfels Castle. Anthony van Dyck painted Charles I and his horse during a break from this action, which is done "in the Snow" in a Bruegel painting.

In a Cranach painting titled for this emotion, a woman whittles away at a stick, ignoring the fifteen babies dancing next to her. Another 16th-century artwork titled for this emotion includes a truncated polyhedron with a faint skull marking on it and a magic square.

bonuses.leadin: Many of this artist’s paintings include his heraldic symbol of a bat-winged serpent. For 10 points each,
parts: Identify this sixteenth-century court painter to the electors of Saxony. He made several paintings of Judith with the head of Holofernes, in which Judith wears a big red hat with feathers.

Cranach’s best known portrait is of this man, who was one of his personal friends. Some art historians have connected Cranach’s interest in the Judith story with this theologian’s ideological struggle against Charles V.

Despite his Lutheran allegiance, Cranach accepted a commission to paint Cardinal Albert von Brandenburg as this figure. His composition was heavily derived from Durer’s woodcut of this saint writing in his study, which features a tame lion in the foreground.
answers_sanitized: Lucas Cranach the Elder [prompt on Cranach]

Cranach's best known portrait is of this man, who was one of his personal friends. Some art historians have connected Cranach's interest in the Judith story with this theologian's ideological struggle against Charles V.

Despite his Lutheran allegiance, Cranach accepted a commission to paint Cardinal Albert von Brandenburg as this figure. His composition was heavily derived from Durer's woodcut of this saint writing in his study, which features a tame lion in the foreground.

bonuses.leadin: This painter spent most of his career employed by the dukes of Saxony. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter best-known for his many portraits of Martin Luther.

Cranach created a famously unusual 1503 painting of this scene, integrating a turbulent landscape and placing Jesus in profile and off to the right. Instead, the focus is on Mary looking up at the recently deceased Christ.

Cranach’s Crucifixion belongs to this German art museum. Other sixteenth-century paintings in this museum include Altdorfer’s Battle of Issus, El Greco’s Disrobing of Christ, and Dürer’s Self Portrait in a Fur-Collared Robe.
answers: the alte Pinakothek (“ALL-tuh PEE-nah-koh-take”), Munich [accept Old Pinacotheca or Old Picture Gallery]
answers_sanitized: the alte Pinakothek ("ALL-tuh PEE-nah-koh-take"), Munich [accept Old Pinacotheca or Old Picture Gallery]

bonuses.leadin: This series’ final entry shows an old man praying in a boat who is made joyous by the appearance of an angel, who gestures towards a light at the top right. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this four-part allegorical painting series which depicts a journey down a river.
This Hudson River school artist of The Voyage of Life also created a five-part series showing the rise and fall of a classical civilization in The Course of Empire.

A pair of Thomas Cole paintings, The Past and The Present, center on one of these buildings that has become ruined over time. Cranach the Elder painted a hunt taking place with one of these in the background near Hartenfels.

bonuses.leadin: This artist’s insignia was a winged black snake holding a ruby ring in its mouth. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Wittenberg-based painter, who depicted the title figures standing in front of a stag and covering their genitals with apple branches in his version of Adam and Eve.
Cranach was a contemporary of this other German artist, with whom he now shares a feast day. This man included a magic square in his Melancolia I.
A skull lies on the window-sill at the left of this Dürer engraving, which features a sleeping lion and dog in the foreground and the title figure seated in the background.
answers: St. Jerome in His Study [or Der heilige Hieronymus im Gehäus]

bonuses.leadin: This man was a pupil of Lucas Cranach the Elder and painted several works housed in St. Florian’s Priory. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this German artist of Landscape with a Footbridge, whose most famous work was commissioned by Duke William IV.
Duke William IV commissioned The Battle of Issus, which depicts this leader defeating the Persians led by Darius III.

Altdorfer also made a version of this scene, which is depicted on the central panel of the Mérode Altarpiece. Other famous versions of this scene include one by Fra Angelico and the Cestello one by Botticelli.

bonuses.leadin: This painter showed two men dressed as medieval knights hanging out with three naked ladies wearing necklaces in his version of The Judgment of Paris. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this "swiftest of painters," who created portraits of contemporary German political figures such as Henry the Pious and Katharina von Mecklenburg.
Perhaps Cranach's most famous subject was this guy, also painted by Hans Holbein. This religious leader was almost always depicted in his floppy black hat.

A lace collar and a nearly invisible draping are the only things worn by the title goddess of this Cranach painting, who has wavy red hair. On a pedestal to the left, it shows the much smaller other title character.

bonuses.leadin: In Cranach the Elder’s version of this scene, the main female figure is wearing a feathered red hat. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this scene which was painted many times by Artemisia Gentileschi. In Caravaggio’s version, a spurt of blood shoots from an Assyrian general’s neck as an old maid holding a sheet looks on.
Adele Bloch-Bauer posed for two paintings of Judith by this Austrian painter of The Kiss, who was friends with Egon Schiele.
Unsurprisingly, Artemisia Gentileschi also painted the scene in which Jael kills this Canaanite general with a tent peg.

bonuses.leadin: Joachim Patinir created a "landscape" with this man, and Cranach the Elder depicted Cardinal Albrecht as him. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this saint depicted "in his study" in an engraving by Albrecht Durer.
In the Durer woodcut, one of these animals, next to a sleeping dog, sits in front of the working Saint Jerome.

Antonello da Messina's Saint Jerome in his Study contains a partridge and one of these creatures in the foreground. These creatures, which symbolize eternal life in Renaissance art, are shown fighting in a room designed for Frederick Leyland.
